purpose: Pure functions partitioning a user's contacts by their relationship to a group (already a member, blocked, or selectable to invite). This story adds the inverse of the existing eligibleGroupsForContact/addableGroupsForContact pair: instead of partitioning groups for one contact, it partitions contacts for one group.

public_api.name: ContactSelectabilityEntry
public_api.signature: type ContactSelectabilityEntry = { contact: ContactListItem; selectable: boolean; disabledReason?: 'already_member' | 'blocked' }
public_api.description: Cross-story seam contract (stories.json#seams) — one partition entry per input contact. disabledReason is present iff selectable is false; 'already_member' takes precedence over 'blocked' when both apply.

public_api.name: selectableContactsForGroup
public_api.signature: function selectableContactsForGroup(contacts: ContactListItem[], group: { memberPubkeys: string[] }): ContactSelectabilityEntry[]
public_api.description: Partitions `contacts` (typically listContacts(ownPubkeyHex, { includeArchived: true }) output) into selectable/disabled-with-reason entries for the given group's memberPubkeys, preserving input order. Case-insensitive membership match (same .toLowerCase() idiom as eligibleGroupsForContact/commonGroups). Pure, synchronous, no storage/network access — the caller supplies both arguments.
